behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watch behavior (recursive watch + OS event delivery for nested paths). A mock watcher can always emit nested events and hide misconfiguration/inode-scope bugs. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only filesystem processing; no AI/ML call or multi-agent sequence, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real daemon against a real temp directory tree, create `root/sub/a.txt`, and assert exactly one transformation output is written for that file within timeout.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly about deduplication logic for same path + time window; this can fail inside one local component (path keying + TTL/window check) without real FS/OS. Rejected higher levels: text says "deduplication logic" and does not require real watcher semantics, network, subprocess, or model output.
behaviors.test_description: Call the dedupe component twice with identical normalized path and controlled clock within the configured window; assert first is accepted and second is rejected, then accepted again after window expiry.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly depends on OS-emitted duplicate events. In-process fakes won’t reproduce watcher/library quirks (rename/change bursts, duplicate callbacks), so mocking can hide real double-processing failures. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent pipeline in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real watcher, perform one write pattern known to emit multiple events (e.g., temp-write+rename), then assert only one pipeline execution/output for that path.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is an OS-level lifecycle case (directory deletion/recreation changes underlying watch target/inode). Module-only tests cannot validate real re-subscription behavior after actual FS removal. Rejected higher levels: behavior is purely filesystem/watch recovery, with no model decision steps.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete it, recreate same path, add a new file, and assert daemon detects and processes it without process restart.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module handoff and ordering logic (event intake -> metadata lookup -> queue ordering -> processor dispatch). This can be caught with wired local modules plus controlled timestamps; real OS behavior is not strictly required to validate ordering policy itself. Rejected higher levels: behavior text specifies deterministic timestamp-based ordering, not real network/subprocess dependence and not AI/ML output.
behaviors.test_description: Wire watcher-handler/queue/processor modules in-process; inject near-simultaneous file events with explicit creation timestamps and assert processing sequence follows timestamp order regardless of event arrival order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real write timing and filesystem event timing (partial writes, close/flush boundaries, size stabilization). A fake writer/event source can mask races and premature pickup bugs. Rejected higher levels: behavior contains no AI/ML decision or chained agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running, stream-write a large file in chunks with delays; assert no processing starts before final chunk/close, then assert processing occurs once after write completion.
